mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce discount rates for equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 6.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and weak consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based domestic demand expansion, generating positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance or forward returns turn negative when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and impending monetary tightening, leading to negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO growth exceeds 2% in late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 2.5 percent year-over-year triggers inflation risk premiums and expectations of rate hikes, producing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of observations when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and corporate earnings growth, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or payroll growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: High real GDP growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strong economic expansion, driving positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Joint signal loses predictive power or yields negative returns when GDP is high and unemployment falls.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial output alongside accelerating inflation during a monetary tightening cycle creates stagflationary pressures, resulting in negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances despite the joint supply-inflation signal.
